incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 2: inefficient choice requiring 30 marks to reach 60

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Scale of 2 would need 30 marks to reach 60. That's a lot of lines. Look at the biggest number in your data.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5: works but Scale of 10 is more efficient for this data range

steps.text: Here's a hint: Scale of 5 could work, but there's a better choice. The biggest number is 60. Which scale gets you there with fewer marks?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights Scale of 10 and shows how it reaches 60 with only 6 marks on the axis

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The biggest number is 60. Scale of 10 gets you there in 6 marks: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. That's cleaner than Scale of 2, which needs 30 marks, or Scale of 5, which needs 12 marks. Scale of 10 is the answer.

or.selected: All values end in 0 or 5 so they can be graphed precisely.
The graph is easy to read.
The biggest number is 60 so the bars fit on the graph.
Scale of 2 takes too many marks to reach the biggest number.

and.selected: All values end in 0 or 5 so they can be graphed precisely.

Scale of 2 takes too many marks to reach the biggest number.

or.selected: Scale of 5 is always the best choice.

incorrects.description: Branch 2: Student selected only correct reasons but not all of them

steps.text: You're getting there. The reasons you picked are valid. Look at the data again. Are there other reasons Scale of 10 works well here?

or.selected: All values end in 0 or 5 so they can be graphed precisely.
The graph is easy to read.

incorrects.description: Branch 3: Student selected no correct reasons

steps.text: Let's take another look. Think about what makes a scale good. Look at the biggest number in the data. What do the numbers end in?

or.selected: All values end in 0 or 5 so they can be graphed precisely.
The graph is easy to read.
The biggest number is 60 so the bars fit on the graph.
Scale of 2 takes too many marks to reach the biggest number.
Scale of 5 is always the best choice.

incorrects.description: Branch 4: Student selected mix of correct and incorrect reasons

steps.text: You're working on it. Some of your choices are right. Check each reason carefully. One or more don't actually explain why Scale of 10 is best for this data.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights each correct reason sequentially: multiples of 5 pattern, readability, fitting 60, and inefficiency of Scale of 2

steps.text: Here, let me walk you through this one. All four reasons are correct. First, all values end in 0 or 5, so they're multiples of 5. Second, fewer marks means easier reading. Third, 60 fits cleanly with Scale of 10 in just 6 marks. Fourth, Scale of 2 would need 30 marks to reach 60. All four reasons explain why Scale of 10 is best.
